steps.title: Identify the protection paradox
steps.content: CLPs are shielded from external conditions by their transmission strategy, yet their abundance decreased as sea temperatures rose, while directly transmitted parasites (exposed to external conditions) remained stable. This contradicts what the shielding advantage would predict.

steps.title: Explain why the advantage didn't prevent decline
steps.content: Choice D explains the paradox: the transmission advantage “did not completely offset the negative effects of other temperature driven factors.” Despite being shielded, CLPs were harmed by other temperature-related causes. The other choices contradict the findings, reverse the host-complexity relationship, or mischaracterize the protection mechanisms. That matches choice D — D is correct.
